title: AI Trust & Safety Digest
tagline: A curated current snapshot of AI trust, safety, and governance, including as many materially important fresh items as the day warrants.
lead: Today’s strongest trust-and-safety signal is practical governance around deployment: one item focuses on platform safeguards and misuse enforcement, while another frames AI as a cybersecurity defense priority. The remaining notable updates are narrower but still relevant, covering data supply-chain accountability and human agency in high-stakes recruiting workflows.

items.title: OpenAI details ChatGPT safety systems, misuse detection, and enforcement
items.why_it_matters: It provides a primary-source view of current deployment safeguards and abuse-response practices for a major frontier system.

items.title: OpenAI proposes five-part plan for AI-era cybersecurity
items.why_it_matters: It treats AI security as critical infrastructure policy, emphasizing defense, resilience, and protection of high-value systems.

items.title: Partnership on AI argues responsible AI begins with the data supply chain
items.why_it_matters: It shifts accountability upstream to data provenance, labor, and governance controls that shape downstream model risk.

items.title: Study examines perceived human agency when GenAI is used in recruiting
items.why_it_matters: It addresses oversight risks in a high-stakes domain where AI assistance can blur responsibility for employment decisions.

items.title: Paper warns of cognitive and systemic failure from AI-dependent software engineering
items.why_it_matters: It highlights reliability and security risks when developers over-rely on AI outputs instead of understanding systems.

items.title: ClawGym introduces a framework for verifiable training data in tool-using agents
items.why_it_matters: It is relevant to agent evaluation because verifiable environments can improve measurement of real-world behavior.

items.title: ClassEval-Pro proposes a cross-domain benchmark for class-level code generation
items.why_it_matters: It adds evaluation coverage for a coding capability that matters for reliability, security review, and deployment risk.

note: Selected from recent trust-and-safety relevant posts and papers by labs, evaluators, standards bodies, policy organizations, and arXiv, then summarized with GPT-5.4. Includes as many materially important fresh items as the day warrants.
